page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs with a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores falling into the top 25% tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark database operations also demonstrate strong performance. Conversely, single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, align with the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show strong performance for large-block read-only operations at 209.21 GB/sec, while write operations and small-block cached reads remain average. Large-model LLM inference is unsupported, and text generation on smaller models exhibits weaker performance.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.hfc6.16xlarge is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and the complete absence of local storage. This makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or applications requiring high-speed local scratch space. However, its dedicated CPU allocation and strong multi-core performance offer high cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded batch processing, CPU-based simulation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the KVM hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core, and lacks local storage and GPU hardware. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core performance, particularly in multi-threaded decompression, PassMark CPU tests, and database operations, though single-core execution and memory write speeds remain average. Due to its low memory-to-CPU ratio and lack of local storage, it is not designed for memory-heavy or local-storage-dependent applications. Instead, it offers a cost-effective profile for compute-bound tasks, making it suitable for multi-threaded batch processing, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.16xlarge features 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring sixty-four high-frequency Intel Xeon cores, designed for demanding multi-threaded processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs running at 3.1 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory yielding 2.0 GB per core
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Strong multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for multi-threaded batch processing and web serving
